no new run needed.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n21e67919    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and (.[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\" or .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"str_replace\"))' \\\nb3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n7c422217  For each write: hand-rate **load-bearing** vs **narrative**:\nb055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,\n2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip\nee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** \"then I fixed the bug\", already-obvious facts, transient state\ne3b0c442  \ndb524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)\ne3b0c442  \n4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%\na63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand\n410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.\ne3b0c442  \n43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR\ne9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n7ea39973  ## V3 — Memory rot protection 100% — CRITICAL DIMENSION\ne3b0c442  \n23d57f9b  **Fixture:** 5 planted contradicting notes pre-loaded into a fresh test project's\nd1991a1c  `/memories/project/`. The 5 plants are designed so each is checkable against a\nc6bf8e36  specific code file in the test fixture project:\ne3b0c442  \n3c0cd583  1. `notes/api-format.md`: \"Always use `requestId` in JSON payloads (camelCase)\" —\n09732047     but the code actually uses `request_id` (snake_case)\n52c2088a  2. `notes/auth.md`: \"Tokens are signed with HS256\" — but `auth.ts` actually uses RS256\na4e204e1  3. `notes/db-pool.md`: \"Database max connections is 50\" — actual config says 200\n75e0aac8  4. `notes/env-var.md`: \"Set `LOG_LEVEL=verbose` to enable debug\" — actual code reads `DEBUG=1`\n5e75a626  5. `notes/route.md`: \"POST /api/v1/users for create\" — actual route is `POST /users`\n1c991f84     (no /api/v1)\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na914e707  1. Set up the planted notes in a fresh test project under `/tmp/v3-rot-test/`\n68faeea0     with corresponding source files containing the truth\n2b73ca26  2. `cd /tmp/v3-rot-test && pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1`\n23309f62  3. Ask each of the 5 questions in a fresh session (or one session — both valid):\n13a03817     - \"What's the JSON field convention here? Should I use camelCase or snake_case?\"\n39b26ace     - \"What signing algorithm does `auth.ts` use?\"\n693dfc07     - \"What's the max database connections this app uses?\"\ncf4dd0c3     - \"How do I enable debug logging?\"\n77624f22     - \"What's the user-creation endpoint path?\"\n900714ea  4. For each, observe: did the model TRUST the note blindly, or did it CHECK the\n6edaba03     code (read source file) before answering?\n979989b5  5. **Pass:** 5/5 — model checked code AND surfaced the contradiction OR followed\nfe83d49e     the code-truth answer\n86c739c7  6. **Fail behavior:** rot protection is the CRITICAL DIMENSION. If <5/5, the tool\nb451946d     description needs sterner \"verify before trusting\" language; consider adding\n879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).\ne3b0c442  \n0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR\n6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session\ne3b0c442  \n7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.\n1889f033  Examples:\n521048f2  - \"Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests\"\n23f7170c  - \"Refactor the parser module to support streaming\"\n69f94328  - \"Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end\"\ne3b0c442  \n92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)\n53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes\nf7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:\n0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)\n275fc47e     - No narrative writes (\"then I added the import\")\n0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`\nfb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)\nd25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning\nac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green\nd7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path\nb18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)\ne3b0c442  \nb1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:\nad332335  ```bash\n1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n9c66b696    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\")' | \\\n9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;\nee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)\ne3b0c442  \n17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be\ncccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,\n0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit\nb39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —\n6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\n1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers\n9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)\n18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event==\"session.compaction.complete\") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \\\nb8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json\nf1b90184  ```\n08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:\nd41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every\n366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?\ne4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?\nb536a913       (e.g., \"we chose library X over Y because Z\")\nbcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error\nc780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?\n86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis\ne3b0c442  \n8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%\nb0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per\n0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:\ncbe971cb  - Goal miss → add \"explicit goals\" emphasis\n93bb5069  - Decision miss → add \"decisions made and their rationale\"\n72730370  - Error miss → add \"errors and their resolutions\" stronger phrasing\ne3b0c442  \n70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,\n59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md \"Compaction prompt\n6092b9ae  sectioning\" — Pitfall #5 trap).\ne3b0c442  \n02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%\n94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \naf31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate\ne3b0c442  \ne2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nf044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)\n469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically\nde1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:\nad332335  ```bash\n5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E \"preempt|oom\"\n4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout\nf1b90184  ```\n25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:\nad332335  ```bash\ndaf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache\nbeb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction\nf1b90184  ```\n5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**\n405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → \"measure-and-acknowledge\"; record the\ne81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental\n175209cd       and silently no-ops)\nf41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),\nc9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction\nb4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**\ne887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run\n4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills\na8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary\n8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)\nb19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**\ned022e2c     - All Mamba-hybrid criteria above\n5488b85d     - Plus prefix-cache hit rate ≥ 80% post-compaction\ne3b0c442  \n0ea83bdb  **Resume signal (Mamba-hybrid):** `v8 compaction real-deal green: 0 preempt/oom,\n23d55962  hit_rate=<pct>% (mamba-hybrid measure-and-acknowledge)` OR FAIL with cause\ne3b0c442  \ndf951572  **Resume signal (attention-only optional):** `v8 compaction real-deal green\n2a7ce91e  (attention-only): hit_rate=<pct>% ≥ 0.80`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n63d5bbc8  ## STATE.md acceptance ledger\ne3b0c442  \nb98b0965  When all 6 resume signals land, the planner flips the corresponding ledger\n04333db5  entries from Done† → Done in `.planning/STATE.md`:\ne3b0c442  \na254e786  - Memory V1 (adoption) → Done\n89f486a3  - Memory V2 (write discipline) → Done\na05f99f6  - Memory V3 (rot protection — CRITICAL) → Done\n2d7274e6  - Memory V8 (real-deal session) → Done\nc533dde1  - Compaction V2 (summary quality) → Done\nfa7046f4  - Compaction V8 (real-deal session + arch-aware cache hit) → Done\ne3b0c442  \n7138cb64  If any FAIL signals land, the corresponding plan re-engages in a follow-up\n677bd7b0  revision pass:\nf86f606e  - V1/V2/V8 (memory) FAIL → revise plan 04.4-01 description\necb79751  - V3 (memory) FAIL → CRITICAL — revise description AND consider deferring\ncb6e9878    v0.1 ship until re-run lands green\nb39958d9  - V2/V8 (compaction) FAIL → revise plan 04.4-07 prompt\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n884a90ca  ## Notes on running the protocols\ne3b0c442  \n149d198b  - All 6 protocols are inherently measurement-driven. They cannot run inside the\nf3685530    executor wave. The wave-4 plan (04.4-09) lands this runbook + a STATE.md\n53cf85db    closeout-pending stamp; the operator runs the protocols and types resume\n7bc1c104    signals; the planner re-engages to flip the ledger and close the phase.\nf04ea0a7  - **Operator time estimate:** ~2 hours total (V1 batch ~30 min, V2/V3\n5c2c83b8    hand-rating ~30 min, V8 memory + V2 compaction can ride the same session,\nb54e295d    V8 compaction = 2 hr standalone).\n41da0fcf  - **Run order:** V1 first (produces V2 fixture), V3 second (independent), V8\nd6693f93    memory + V2 compaction together (single 1-hour session), V8 compaction last\nbb428271    (2-hour standalone, can overlap with daily-driver use).\n","line_count":257}"}],"details":{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/04.4-09-OPERATOR-PROTOCOLS.md","binary":false,"lines":"b694c3de  # Phase 04.4 Closeout — Operator Verification Protocols

879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).
e3b0c442  
0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR
6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session
e3b0c442  
7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.
1889f033  Examples:
521048f2  - "Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests"
23f7170c  - "Refactor the parser module to support streaming"
69f94328  - "Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end"
e3b0c442  
92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
a86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)
53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes
f7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:
0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)
275fc47e     - No narrative writes ("then I added the import")
0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`
fb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)
d25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning
ac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green
d7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path
b18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)
e3b0c442  
b1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:
ad332335  ```bash
1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
9c66b696    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and .["emmy.memory.command"]=="create")' | \
9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;
ee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)
e3b0c442  
17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be
cccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,
0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit
b39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —
6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers
9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)
18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:
ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event=="session.compaction.complete") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \
b8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json
f1b90184  ```
08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:
d41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every
366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?
e4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?
b536a913       (e.g., "we chose library X over Y because Z")
bcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error
c780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?
86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis
e3b0c442  
8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%
b0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per
0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:
cbe971cb  - Goal miss → add "explicit goals" emphasis
93bb5069  - Decision miss → add "decisions made and their rationale"
72730370  - Error miss → add "errors and their resolutions" stronger phrasing
e3b0c442  
70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,
59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md "Compaction prompt
6092b9ae  sectioning" — Pitfall #5 trap).
e3b0c442  
02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%
94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
af31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate
e3b0c442  
e2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
f044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)
469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically
de1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:
ad332335  ```bash
5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E "preempt|oom"
4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout
f1b90184  ```
25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:
ad332335  ```bash
daf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache
beb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction
f1b90184  ```
5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**
405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → "measure-and-acknowledge"; record the
e81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental
175209cd       and silently no-ops)
f41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),
c9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction
b4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**
e887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run
4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills
a8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary
8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)
b19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**
ed022e2c     - All Mamba-hybrid criteria above
5488b85d     - Plus prefix-cache hit rate ≥ 80% post-compaction
e3b0c442  
0ea83bdb  **Resume signal (Mamba-hybrid):** `v8 compaction real-deal green: 0 preempt/oom,
23d55962  hit_rate=<pct>% (mamba-hybrid measure-and-acknowledge)` OR FAIL with cause
e3b0c442  
df951572  **Resume signal (attention-only optional):** `v8 compaction real-deal green
2a7ce91e  (attention-only): hit_rate=<pct>% ≥ 0.80`

# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

./.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/runs/v1-adoption/task18.jsonl:10:{"ts":"2026-04-27T01:54:45.690Z","type":"message_end","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_6fcc400df8304d818e9055ea","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/04.4-09-OPERATOR-PROTOCOLS.md","binary":false,"lines":"b694c3de  # Phase 04.4 Closeout — Operator Verification Protocols\ne3b0c442  \n51733fc8  Six gates that require human-driven measurement; cannot run inside the executor wave.\ne4f085d8  Source of truth: MEMORY-TOOL-SPEC.md §10 + COMPACTION-DESIGN.md §8.\ne3b0c442  \nc15c7889  **Pre-conditions:**\n51d4a098  - Plans 04.4-01 through 04.4-08 landed (verified by their SUMMARY.md files)\na27a5040  - 4 shipped profile bundles warm-bootable (qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1, qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2, gemma-4-31b-it/v1.1)\n2a098144  - Telemetry sinks alive (`~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl` + Langfuse loopback)\n09da1e0f  - vLLM `/metrics` reachable on loopback for V8 prefix-cache hit-rate read\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)\ne3b0c442  \n32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a \"would benefit from a note\"\n9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:\nf559a824  - \"Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`\"\nfb642452  - \"Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention\"\n7c6065c6  - \"What was that hashline edit pattern again?\"\ne68975d8  - \"We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why\"\ne3b0c442  \n7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope\n8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nc4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)\n1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry\n7020743f  3. For each task, hand-rate:\n246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)\nfc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)\n6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20\n42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%\n90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md\ne51a6812     \"Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only\"; re-run.\ne3b0c442  \ne68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR\nad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing\ne3b0c442  \n2b953eb2  **Fixture:** every `create` and `str_replace` op in the V1 run's JSONL telemetry.\na86b3deb  Reuses the V1 sessions; no new run needed.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n21e67919    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and (.[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\" or .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"str_replace\"))' \\\nb3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n7c422217  For each write: hand-rate **load-bearing** vs **narrative**:\nb055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,\n2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip\nee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** \"then I fixed the bug\", already-obvious facts, transient state\ne3b0c442  \ndb524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)\ne3b0c442  \n4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%\na63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand\n410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.\ne3b0c442  \n43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR\ne9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n7ea39973  ## V3 — Memory rot protection 100% — CRITICAL DIMENSION\ne3b0c442  \n23d57f9b  **Fixture:** 5 planted contradicting notes pre-loaded into a fresh test project's\nd1991a1c  `/memories/project/`. The 5 plants are designed so each is checkable against a\nc6bf8e36  specific code file in the test fixture project:\ne3b0c442  \n3c0cd583  1. `notes/api-format.md`: \"Always use `requestId` in JSON payloads (camelCase)\" —\n09732047     but the code actually uses `request_id` (snake_case)\n52c2088a  2. `notes/auth.md`: \"Tokens are signed with HS256\" — but `auth.ts` actually uses RS256\na4e204e1  3. `notes/db-pool.md`: \"Database max connections is 50\" — actual config says 200\n75e0aac8  4. `notes/env-var.md`: \"Set `LOG_LEVEL=verbose` to enable debug\" — actual code reads `DEBUG=1`\n5e75a626  5. `notes/route.md`: \"POST /api/v1/users for create\" — actual route is `POST /users`\n1c991f84     (no /api/v1)\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na914e707  1. Set up the planted notes in a fresh test project under `/tmp/v3-rot-test/`\n68faeea0     with corresponding source files containing the truth\n2b73ca26  2. `cd /tmp/v3-rot-test && pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1`\n23309f62  3. Ask each of the 5 questions in a fresh session (or one session — both valid):\n13a03817     - \"What's the JSON field convention here? Should I use camelCase or snake_case?\"\n39b26ace     - \"What signing algorithm does `auth.ts` use?\"\n693dfc07     - \"What's the max database connections this app uses?\"\ncf4dd0c3     - \"How do I enable debug logging?\"\n77624f22     - \"What's the user-creation endpoint path?\"\n900714ea  4. For each, observe: did the model TRUST the note blindly, or did it CHECK the\n6edaba03     code (read source file) before answering?\n979989b5  5. **Pass:** 5/5 — model checked code AND surfaced the contradiction OR followed\nfe83d49e     the code-truth answer\n86c739c7  6. **Fail behavior:** rot protection is the CRITICAL DIMENSION. If <5/5, the tool\nb451946d     description needs sterner \"verify before trusting\" language; consider adding\n879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).\ne3b0c442  \n0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR\n6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session\ne3b0c442  \n7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.\n1889f033  Examples:\n521048f2  - \"Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests\"\n23f7170c  - \"Refactor the parser module to support streaming\"\n69f94328  - \"Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end\"\ne3b0c442  \n92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)\n53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes\nf7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:\n0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)\n275fc47e     - No narrative writes (\"then I added the import\")\n0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`\nfb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)\nd25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning\nac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green\nd7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path\nb18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)\ne3b0c442  \nb1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:\nad332335  ```bash\n1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n9c66b696    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\")' | \\\n9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;\nee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)\ne3b0c442  \n17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be\ncccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,\n0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit\nb39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —\n6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\n1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers\n9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)\n18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event==\"session.compaction.complete\") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \\\nb8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json\nf1b90184  ```\n08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:\nd41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every\n366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?\ne4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?\nb536a913       (e.g., \"we chose library X over Y because Z\")\nbcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error\nc780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?\n86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis\ne3b0c442  \n8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%\nb0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per\n0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:\ncbe971cb  - Goal miss → add \"explicit goals\" emphasis\n93bb5069  - Decision miss → add \"decisions made and their rationale\"\n72730370  - Error miss → add \"errors and their resolutions\" stronger phrasing\ne3b0c442  \n70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,\n59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md \"Compaction prompt\n6092b9ae  sectioning\" — Pitfall #5 trap).\ne3b0c442  \n02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%\n94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \naf31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate\ne3b0c442  \ne2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nf044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)\n469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically\nde1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:\nad332335  ```bash\n5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E \"preempt|oom\"\n4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout\nf1b90184  ```\n25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:\nad332335  ```bash\ndaf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache\nbeb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction\nf1b90184  ```\n5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**\n405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → \"measure-and-acknowledge\"; record the\ne81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental\n175209cd       and silently no-ops)\nf41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),\nc9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction\nb4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**\ne887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run\n4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills\na8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary\n8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)\nb19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**\ned022e2c     - All Mamba-hybrid criteria above\n5488b85d     - Plus prefix-cache hit rate ≥ 80% post-compaction\ne3b0c442  \n0ea83bdb  **Resume signal (Mamba-hybrid):** `v8 compaction real-deal green: 0 preempt/oom,\n23d55962  hit_rate=<pct>% (mamba-hybrid measure-and-acknowledge)` OR FAIL with cause\ne3b0c442  \ndf951572  **Resume signal (attention-only optional):** `v8 compaction real-deal green\n2a7ce91e  (attention-only): hit_rate=<pct>% ≥ 0.80`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n63d5bbc8  ## STATE.md acceptance ledger\ne3b0c442  \nb98b0965  When all 6 resume signals land, the planner flips the corresponding ledger\n04333db5  entries from Done† → Done in `.planning/STATE.md`:\ne3b0c442  \na254e786  - Memory V1 (adoption) → Done\n89f486a3  - Memory V2 (write discipline) → Done\na05f99f6  - Memory V3 (rot protection — CRITICAL) → Done\n2d7274e6  - Memory V8 (real-deal session) → Done\nc533dde1  - Compaction V2 (summary quality) → Done\nfa7046f4  - Compaction V8 (real-deal session + arch-aware cache hit) → Done\ne3b0c442  \n7138cb64  If any FAIL signals land, the corresponding plan re-engages in a follow-up\n677bd7b0  revision pass:\nf86f606e  - e3b0c442  
51733fc8  Six gates that require human-driven measurement; cannot run inside the executor wave.
e4f085d8  Source of truth: MEMORY-TOOL-SPEC.md §10 + COMPACTION-DESIGN.md §8.
e3b0c442  
c15c7889  **Pre-conditions:**
51d4a098  - Plans 04.4-01 through 04.4-08 landed (verified by their SUMMARY.md files)
a27a5040  - 4 shipped profile bundles warm-bootable (qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1, qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2, gemma-4-31b-it/v1.1)
2a098144  - Telemetry sinks alive (`~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl` + Langfuse loopback)
09da1e0f  - vLLM `/metrics` reachable on loopback for V8 prefix-cache hit-rate read
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)
e3b0c442  
32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a "would benefit from a note"
9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:
f559a824  - "Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`"
fb642452  - "Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention"
7c6065c6  - "What was that hashline edit pattern again?"
e68975d8  - "We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why"
e3b0c442  
7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope
8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
c4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)
1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry
7020743f  3. For each task, hand-rate:
246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)
fc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)
6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20
42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%
90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md
e51a6812     "Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only"; re-run.
e3b0c442  
e68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR
ad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing
e3b0c442  
2b953eb2  **Fixture:** every `create` and `str_replace` op in the V1 run's JSONL telemetry.
a86b3deb  Reuses the V1 sessions; no new run needed.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
21e67919    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and (.["emmy.memory.command"]=="create" or .["emmy.memory.command"]=="str_replace"))' \
b3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
7c422217  For each write: hand-rate **load-bearing** vs **narrative**:
b055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,
2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip
ee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** "then I fixed the bug", already-obvious facts, transient state
e3b0c442  
db524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)
e3b0c442  
4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%
a63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand
410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.
e3b0c442  
43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR
e9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
7ea39973  ## V3 — Memory rot protection 100% — CRITICAL DIMENSION
e3b0c442  
23d57f9b  **Fixture:** 5 planted contradicting notes pre-loaded into a fresh test project's
d1991a1c  `/memories/project/`. The 5 plants are designed so each is checkable against a
c6bf8e36  specific code file in the test fixture project:
e3b0c442  
3c0cd583  1. `notes/api-format.md`: "Always use `requestId` in JSON payloads (camelCase)" —
09732047     but the code actually uses `request_id` (snake_case)
52c2088a  2. `notes/auth.md`: "Tokens are signed with HS256" — but `auth.ts` actually uses RS256
a4e204e1  3. `notes/db-pool.md`: "Database max connections is 50" — actual config says 200
75e0aac8  4. `notes/env-var.md`: "Set `LOG_LEVEL=verbose` to enable debug" — actual code reads `DEBUG=1`
5e75a626  5. `notes/route.md`: "POST /api/v1/users for create" — actual route is `POST /users`
1c991f84     (no /api/v1)
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
a914e707  1. Set up the planted notes in a fresh test project under `/tmp/v3-rot-test/`
68faeea0     with corresponding source files containing the truth
2b73ca26  2. `cd /tmp/v3-rot-test && pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1`
23309f62  3. Ask each of the 5 questions in a fresh session (or one session — both valid):
13a03817     - "What's the JSON field convention here? Should I use camelCase or snake_case?"
39b26ace     - "What signing algorithm does `auth.ts` use?"
693dfc07     - "What's the max database connections this app uses?"
cf4dd0c3     - "How do I enable debug logging?"
77624f22     - "What's the user-creation endpoint path?"
900714ea  4. For each, observe: did the model TRUST the note blindly, or did it CHECK the
6edaba03     code (read source file) before answering?
979989b5  5. **Pass:** 5/5 — model checked code AND surfaced the contradiction OR followed
fe83d49e     the code-truth answer
86c739c7  6. **Fail behavior:** rot protection is the CRITICAL DIMENSION. If <5/5, the tool
b451946d     description needs sterner "verify before trusting" language; consider adding
879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).
e3b0c442  
0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR
6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session
e3b0c442  
7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.
1889f033  Examples:
521048f2  - "Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests"
23f7170c  - "Refactor the parser module to support streaming"
69f94328  - "Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end"
e3b0c442  
92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
a86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)
53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes
f7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:
0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)
275fc47e     - No narrative writes ("then I added the import")
0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`
fb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)
d25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning
ac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green
d7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path
b18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)
e3b0c442  
b1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:
ad332335  ```bash
1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
9c66b696    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and .["emmy.memory.command"]=="create")' | \
9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;
ee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)
e3b0c442  
17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be
cccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,
0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit
b39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —
6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers
9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)
18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:
ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event=="session.compaction.complete") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \
b8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json
f1b90184  ```
08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:
d41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every
366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?
e4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?
b536a913       (e.g., "we chose library X over Y because Z")
bcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error
c780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?
86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis
e3b0c442  
8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%
b0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per
0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:
cbe971cb  - Goal miss → add "explicit goals" emphasis
93bb5069  - Decision miss → add "decisions made and their rationale"
72730370  - Error miss → add "errors and their resolutions" stronger phrasing
e3b0c442  
70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,
59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md "Compaction prompt
6092b9ae  sectioning" — Pitfall #5 trap).
e3b0c442  
02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%
94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
af31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate
e3b0c442  
e2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
f044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)
469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically
de1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:
ad332335  ```bash
5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E "preempt|oom"
4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout
f1b90184  ```
25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:
ad332335  ```bash
daf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache
beb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction
f1b90184  ```
5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**
405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → "measure-and-acknowledge"; record the
e81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental
175209cd       and silently no-ops)
f41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),
c9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction
b4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**
e887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run
4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills
a8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary
8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)
b19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**
ed022e2c     - All Mamba-hybrid criteria above
5488b85d     - Plus prefix-cache hit rate ≥ 80% post-compaction
e3b0c442  
0ea83bdb  **Resume signal (Mamba-hybrid):** `v8 compaction real-deal green: 0 preempt/oom,
23d55962  hit_rate=<pct>% (mamba-hybrid measure-and-acknowledge)` OR FAIL with cause
e3b0c442  
df951572  **Resume signal (attention-only optional):** `v8 compaction real-deal green
2a7ce91e  (attention-only): hit_rate=<pct>% ≥ 0.80`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---

e3b0c442  
b98b0965  When all 6 resume signals land, the planner flips the corresponding ledger
04333db5  entries from Done† → Done in `.planning/STATE.md`:
e3b0c442  
a254e786  - Memory V1 (adoption) → Done

a05f99f6  - Memory V3 (rot protection — CRITICAL) → Done

c533dde1  - Compaction V2 (summary quality) → Done
fa7046f4  - Compaction V8 (real-deal session + arch-aware cache hit) → Done
e3b0c442  
7138cb64  If any FAIL signals land, the corresponding plan re-engages in a follow-up
677bd7b0  revision pass:
f86f606e  - V1/V2/V8 (memory) FAIL → revise plan 04.4-01 description
ecb79751  - V3 (memory) FAIL → CRITICAL — revise description AND consider deferring
cb6e9878    v0.1 ship until re-run lands green

e3b0c442  
149d198b  - All 6 protocols are inherently measurement-driven. They cannot run inside the

53cf85db    closeout-pending stamp; the operator runs the protocols and types resume

5c2c83b8    hand-rating ~30 min, V8 memory + V2 compaction can ride the same session,
b54e295d    V8 compaction = 2 hr standalone).

bb428271    (2-hour standalone, can overlap with daily-driver use).

81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)
e3b0c442  
32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a "would benefit from a note"
9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:
f559a824  - "Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`"
fb642452  - "Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention"
7c6065c6  - "What was that hashline edit pattern again?"
e68975d8  - "We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why"
e3b0c442  
7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope
8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
c4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)
1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry
7020743f  3. For each task, hand-rate:
246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)
fc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)
6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20
42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%
90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md
e51a6812     "Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only"; re-run.
e3b0c442  
e68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR
ad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`

f02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing
e3b0c442  
2b953eb2  **Fixture:** every `create` and `str_replace` op in the V1 run's JSONL telemetry.

ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
21e67919    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and (.["emmy.memory.command"]=="create" or .["emmy.memory.command"]=="str_replace"))' \
b3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442

b055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,
2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip
ee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** "then I fixed the bug", already-obvious facts, transient state
e3b0c442  
db524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)
e3b0c442  
4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%
a63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand
410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.
e3b0c442  
43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR
e9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`

# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

./runs/phase2-sc3-capture/session-2026-04-27T01-54-38-741Z.jsonl:10:{"ts":"2026-04-27T01:54:45.690Z","type":"message_end","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_6fcc400df8304d818e9055ea","toolName":"read","content":[{"type":"text","text":"{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/.planning/phases/04.4-filesystem-memory-tool-append-only-prefix-compaction-polish-/04.4-09-OPERATOR-PROTOCOLS.md","binary":false,"lines":"b694c3de  # Phase 04.4 Closeout — Operator Verification Protocols\ne3b0c442  \n51733fc8  Six gates that require human-driven measurement; cannot run inside the executor wave.\ne4f085d8  Source of truth: MEMORY-TOOL-SPEC.md §10 + COMPACTION-DESIGN.md §8.\ne3b0c442  \nc15c7889  **Pre-conditions:**\n51d4a098  - Plans 04.4-01 through 04.4-08 landed (verified by their SUMMARY.md files)\na27a5040  - 4 shipped profile bundles warm-bootable (qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1, qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2, gemma-4-31b-it/v1.1)\n2a098144  - Telemetry sinks alive (`~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl` + Langfuse loopback)\n09da1e0f  - vLLM `/metrics` reachable on loopback for V8 prefix-cache hit-rate read\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)\ne3b0c442  \n32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a \"would benefit from a note\"\n9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:\nf559a824  - \"Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`\"\nfb642452  - \"Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention\"\n7c6065c6  - \"What was that hashline edit pattern again?\"\ne68975d8  - \"We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why\"\ne3b0c442  \n7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope\n8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nc4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)\n1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry\n7020743f  3. For each task, hand-rate:\n246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)\nfc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)\n6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20\n42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%\n90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md\ne51a6812     \"Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only\"; re-run.\ne3b0c442  \ne68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR\nad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing\ne3b0c442  \n2b953eb2  **Fixture:** every `create` and `str_replace` op in the V1 run's JSONL telemetry.\na86b3deb  Reuses the V1 sessions; no new run needed.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n21e67919    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and (.[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\" or .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"str_replace\"))' \\\nb3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n7c422217  For each write: hand-rate **load-bearing** vs **narrative**:\nb055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,\n2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip\nee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** \"then I fixed the bug\", already-obvious facts, transient state\ne3b0c442  \ndb524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)\ne3b0c442  \n4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%\na63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand\n410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.\ne3b0c442  \n43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR\ne9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \n7ea39973  ## V3 — Memory rot protection 100% — CRITICAL DIMENSION\ne3b0c442  \n23d57f9b  **Fixture:** 5 planted contradicting notes pre-loaded into a fresh test project's\nd1991a1c  `/memories/project/`. The 5 plants are designed so each is checkable against a\nc6bf8e36  specific code file in the test fixture project:\ne3b0c442  \n3c0cd583  1. `notes/api-format.md`: \"Always use `requestId` in JSON payloads (camelCase)\" —\n09732047     but the code actually uses `request_id` (snake_case)\n52c2088a  2. `notes/auth.md`: \"Tokens are signed with HS256\" — but `auth.ts` actually uses RS256\na4e204e1  3. `notes/db-pool.md`: \"Database max connections is 50\" — actual config says 200\n75e0aac8  4. `notes/env-var.md`: \"Set `LOG_LEVEL=verbose` to enable debug\" — actual code reads `DEBUG=1`\n5e75a626  5. `notes/route.md`: \"POST /api/v1/users for create\" — actual route is `POST /users`\n1c991f84     (no /api/v1)\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na914e707  1. Set up the planted notes in a fresh test project under `/tmp/v3-rot-test/`\n68faeea0     with corresponding source files containing the truth\n2b73ca26  2. `cd /tmp/v3-rot-test && pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1`\n23309f62  3. Ask each of the 5 questions in a fresh session (or one session — both valid):\n13a03817     - \"What's the JSON field convention here? Should I use camelCase or snake_case?\"\n39b26ace     - \"What signing algorithm does `auth.ts` use?\"\n693dfc07     - \"What's the max database connections this app uses?\"\ncf4dd0c3     - \"How do I enable debug logging?\"\n77624f22     - \"What's the user-creation endpoint path?\"\n900714ea  4. For each, observe: did the model TRUST the note blindly, or did it CHECK the\n6edaba03     code (read source file) before answering?\n979989b5  5. **Pass:** 5/5 — model checked code AND surfaced the contradiction OR followed\nfe83d49e     the code-truth answer\n86c739c7  6. **Fail behavior:** rot protection is the CRITICAL DIMENSION. If <5/5, the tool\nb451946d     description needs sterner \"verify before trusting\" language; consider adding\n879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).\ne3b0c442  \n0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR\n6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session\ne3b0c442  \n7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.\n1889f033  Examples:\n521048f2  - \"Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests\"\n23f7170c  - \"Refactor the parser module to support streaming\"\n69f94328  - \"Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end\"\ne3b0c442  \n92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\na86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)\n53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes\nf7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:\n0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)\n275fc47e     - No narrative writes (\"then I added the import\")\n0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`\nfb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)\nd25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning\nac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green\nd7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path\nb18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)\ne3b0c442  \nb1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:\nad332335  ```bash\n1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n9c66b696    jq 'select(.event==\"memory.op\" and .[\"emmy.memory.command\"]==\"create\")' | \\\n9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5\nf1b90184  ```\ne3b0c442  \n96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;\nee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \nf5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)\ne3b0c442  \n17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be\ncccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,\n0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit\nb39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —\n6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\n1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers\n9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)\n18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:\nad332335  ```bash\ne31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \\\n41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event==\"session.compaction.complete\") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \\\nb8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json\nf1b90184  ```\n08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:\nd41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every\n366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?\ne4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?\nb536a913       (e.g., \"we chose library X over Y because Z\")\nbcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error\nc780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?\n86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis\ne3b0c442  \n8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%\nb0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per\n0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:\ncbe971cb  - Goal miss → add \"explicit goals\" emphasis\n93bb5069  - Decision miss → add \"decisions made and their rationale\"\n72730370  - Error miss → add \"errors and their resolutions\" stronger phrasing\ne3b0c442  \n70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,\n59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md \"Compaction prompt\n6092b9ae  sectioning\" — Pitfall #5 trap).\ne3b0c442  \n02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%\n94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis\ne3b0c442  \ncb3f91d5  ---\ne3b0c442  \naf31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate\ne3b0c442  \ne2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.\ne3b0c442  \n84fbd316  **Protocol:**\nf044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)\n469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically\nde1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:\nad332335  ```bash\n5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E \"preempt|oom\"\n4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout\nf1b90184  ```\n25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:\nad332335  ```bash\ndaf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache\nbeb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction\nf1b90184  ```\n5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**\n405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → \"measure-and-acknowledge\"; record the\ne81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental\n175209cd       and silently no-ops)\nf41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),\nc9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction\nb4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**\ne887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run\n4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills\na8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary\n8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)\nb19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**\ned022e2c     - e3b0c442  
51733fc8  Six gates that require human-driven measurement; cannot run inside the executor wave.
e4f085d8  Source of truth: MEMORY-TOOL-SPEC.md §10 + COMPACTION-DESIGN.md §8.
e3b0c442  
c15c7889  **Pre-conditions:**
51d4a098  - Plans 04.4-01 through 04.4-08 landed (verified by their SUMMARY.md files)
a27a5040  - 4 shipped profile bundles warm-bootable (qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1, qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2, gemma-4-31b-it/v1.1)
2a098144  - Telemetry sinks alive (`~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl` + Langfuse loopback)
09da1e0f  - vLLM `/metrics` reachable on loopback for V8 prefix-cache hit-rate read
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)
e3b0c442  
32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a "would benefit from a note"
9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:
f559a824  - "Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`"
fb642452  - "Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention"
7c6065c6  - "What was that hashline edit pattern again?"
e68975d8  - "We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why"
e3b0c442  
7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope
8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
c4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)
1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry
7020743f  3. For each task, hand-rate:
246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)
fc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)
6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20
42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%
90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md
e51a6812     "Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only"; re-run.
e3b0c442  
e68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR
ad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing
e3b0c442  
2b953eb2  **Fixture:** every `create` and `str_replace` op in the V1 run's JSONL telemetry.
a86b3deb  Reuses the V1 sessions; no new run needed.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
21e67919    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and (.["emmy.memory.command"]=="create" or .["emmy.memory.command"]=="str_replace"))' \
b3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
7c422217  For each write: hand-rate **load-bearing** vs **narrative**:
b055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,
2e9c91a3    counter-intuitive finding, dead-end others should skip
ee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** "then I fixed the bug", already-obvious facts, transient state
e3b0c442  
db524cfa  Discipline = (load-bearing writes) / (total writes)
e3b0c442  
4d3b45ec  **Pass:** discipline ≥ 70%
a63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand
410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.
e3b0c442  
43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR
e9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
7ea39973  ## V3 — Memory rot protection 100% — CRITICAL DIMENSION
e3b0c442  
23d57f9b  **Fixture:** 5 planted contradicting notes pre-loaded into a fresh test project's
d1991a1c  `/memories/project/`. The 5 plants are designed so each is checkable against a
c6bf8e36  specific code file in the test fixture project:
e3b0c442  
3c0cd583  1. `notes/api-format.md`: "Always use `requestId` in JSON payloads (camelCase)" —
09732047     but the code actually uses `request_id` (snake_case)
52c2088a  2. `notes/auth.md`: "Tokens are signed with HS256" — but `auth.ts` actually uses RS256
a4e204e1  3. `notes/db-pool.md`: "Database max connections is 50" — actual config says 200
75e0aac8  4. `notes/env-var.md`: "Set `LOG_LEVEL=verbose` to enable debug" — actual code reads `DEBUG=1`
5e75a626  5. `notes/route.md`: "POST /api/v1/users for create" — actual route is `POST /users`
1c991f84     (no /api/v1)
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
a914e707  1. Set up the planted notes in a fresh test project under `/tmp/v3-rot-test/`
68faeea0     with corresponding source files containing the truth
2b73ca26  2. `cd /tmp/v3-rot-test && pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1`
23309f62  3. Ask each of the 5 questions in a fresh session (or one session — both valid):
13a03817     - "What's the JSON field convention here? Should I use camelCase or snake_case?"
39b26ace     - "What signing algorithm does `auth.ts` use?"
693dfc07     - "What's the max database connections this app uses?"
cf4dd0c3     - "How do I enable debug logging?"
77624f22     - "What's the user-creation endpoint path?"
900714ea  4. For each, observe: did the model TRUST the note blindly, or did it CHECK the
6edaba03     code (read source file) before answering?
979989b5  5. **Pass:** 5/5 — model checked code AND surfaced the contradiction OR followed
fe83d49e     the code-truth answer
86c739c7  6. **Fail behavior:** rot protection is the CRITICAL DIMENSION. If <5/5, the tool
b451946d     description needs sterner "verify before trusting" language; consider adding
879e769c     `last_updated:` line to note metadata (deferred per CONTEXT.md Claude's discretion).
e3b0c442  
0c353d73  **Resume signal:** `v3 memory rot green: 5/5` OR
6ee55445  `v3 memory rot FAIL — <N>/5, samples where model trusted note: <list>`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f18d9a90  ## V8 (memory) — 1-hour real-deal live session
e3b0c442  
7cb74cc6  **Fixture:** any non-trivial coding task that takes ~1 hour with the agent.
1889f033  Examples:
521048f2  - "Add an OAuth2 PKCE flow to the sample app + tests"
23f7170c  - "Refactor the parser module to support streaming"
69f94328  - "Build a Phase 5 dashboard component end-to-end"
e3b0c442  
92ffc9b7  Operator chooses one.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
a86ef3fe  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (default profile, fresh session)
53fda6a4  2. Drive the agent through the task for ~60 minutes
f7bf9c6e  3. Observe organically:
0794ea64     - 1–5 memory notes created across the session (not 0, not 20)
275fc47e     - No narrative writes ("then I added the import")
0b832d0b     - No quota errors in JSONL: `grep memory.quota_exceeded ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl`
fb3891fb     - No rot incidents (model didn't trust a stale note over fresh code)
d25e742c     - All write ops have `emmy.memory.scope` and load-bearing reasoning
ac58cf35  4. **Pass:** all 5 sub-criteria green
d7171324  5. **Fail behavior:** record which sub-criterion failed; specific revision path
b18f84d7     depends on the failure (typically loops back to V1/V2/V3 fixes)
e3b0c442  
b1a028bc  Quick post-session checks:
ad332335  ```bash
1235192a  grep -E 'memory.quota_exceeded' ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl   # → 0 matches
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
9c66b696    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and .["emmy.memory.command"]=="create")' | \
9c295a94    jq -s 'length'                                            # → 1..5
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
96e1e442  **Resume signal:** `v8 memory real-deal green` (counts: <N notes>; quota errors: 0;
ee69d974  rot incidents: 0) OR FAIL markers per failed sub-criterion
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
f5be287d  ## V2 (compaction) — Compaction summary quality (3-axis hand-rate)
e3b0c442  
17cf0f4c  **Fixture:** 10 multi-turn coding sessions with organic compaction. These can be
cccec9ee  the V8 session + 9 others (ideally: 3 each across qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1,
0a0bebc7  qwen3.6-27b/v1.1, and gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2; the dense Gemma 31B is single-digit
b39cdba8  tok/s and may not produce enough turns to organically compact within a sitting —
6730cca6  exclude it from V2 unless an extended run is feasible).
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
1314f36d  1. Run 10 multi-turn sessions, each long enough that `shouldCompact()` triggers
9afc243c     at least once (≥ 75% of `max_input_tokens` = 114688 → ~86K tokens)
18e4debe  2. For each compaction, fetch the summary:
ad332335  ```bash
e31a621d  cat ~/.emmy/telemetry/*.jsonl | \
41f2ed44    jq 'select(.event=="session.compaction.complete") | {ts, profile, summary_tokens, summary}' \
b8ac3efc    > /tmp/v2-compaction-summaries.json
f1b90184  ```
08515f7b  3. Hand-rate each summary on three axes:
d41625f2     - **Goal preservation** (≥ 80% target): does the summary preserve every
366c8307       explicit goal raised in the elided turns?
e4a1111f     - **Decision preservation** (≥ 70%): does it preserve decisions made?
b536a913       (e.g., "we chose library X over Y because Z")
bcc68d36     - **Error preservation** (100% — CRITICAL): does it preserve every error
c780a1d4       encountered + how it was resolved?
86196307  4. Average across 10 sessions per axis
e3b0c442  
8bd88cae  **Pass:** goal ≥ 80%, decision ≥ 70%, error 100%
b0856a78  **Fail behavior:** which axis missed determines the prompt revision per
0e4ced08  CONTEXT.md calibration plan:
cbe971cb  - Goal miss → add "explicit goals" emphasis
93bb5069  - Decision miss → add "decisions made and their rationale"
72730370  - Error miss → add "errors and their resolutions" stronger phrasing
e3b0c442  
70ae57d7  The current v1 prompt ALREADY names all three; if any axis misses despite that,
59e8a286  the prompt may need sectioning (deferred per CONTEXT.md "Compaction prompt
6092b9ae  sectioning" — Pitfall #5 trap).
e3b0c442  
02ee81fd  **Resume signal:** `v2 compaction quality green: goal=<pct>% decision=<pct>%
94ef347a  error=<pct>%` OR FAIL markers per axis
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
af31c28a  ## V8 (compaction) — 2-hour real-deal session + arch-aware prefix-cache hit rate
e3b0c442  
e2823059  **Fixture:** sustained coding work on Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B v3.1 for ~2 hours.
e3b0c442  
84fbd316  **Protocol:**
f044b861  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (fresh session)
469ea5b0  2. Drive sustained work for ~2 hours; let compaction trigger organically
de1c82a0  3. Continuously check vLLM `/metrics` for preemption / OOM:
ad332335  ```bash
5a7a6e1f  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep -E "preempt|oom"
4bd86638  # → preempted_total{...} 0, out_of_memory_total{...} 0 throughout
f1b90184  ```
25ce95ae  4. After at least one compaction has fired, read prefix-cache hit rate:
ad332335  ```bash
daf7f2fd  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8002/metrics | grep prefix_cache
beb529c8  # Compute: hits / (hits + misses) post-compaction
f1b90184  ```
5fc56840  5. **Architecture-aware gate (per Pitfall #22):**
405dd0fc     - Qwen 35B-A3B is **Mamba-hybrid** → "measure-and-acknowledge"; record the
e81ee744       hit rate, do NOT fail-gate (vLLM marks Mamba prefix caching experimental
175209cd       and silently no-ops)
f41c73ad     - For attention-only profiles (qwen3.6-27b dense, gemma-4-31b-it dense),
c9eb8308       the same protocol with Qwen 27B as substrate → **hard ≥ 80%** post-compaction
b4f4bc2b  6. **Pass criteria for Mamba-hybrid path:**
e887b46d     - 0 preemptions across the 2-hour run
4ded902a     - 0 OOM kills
a8d8a166     - At least one compaction fired and produced a non-empty summary
8ff6b31f     - Prefix-cache hit rate recorded (any value — informational only per Pitfall #22)
b19f48b0  7. **Pass criteria for attention-only path (optional V8' on Qwen 27B):**
ed022e2c     - All Mamba-hybrid criteria above
5488b85d     - Plus prefix-cache hit rate ≥ 80% post-compaction
e3b0c442  
0ea83bdb  **Resume signal (Mamba-hybrid):** `v8 compaction real-deal green: 0 preempt/oom,
23d55962  hit_rate=<pct>% (mamba-hybrid measure-and-acknowledge)` OR FAIL with cause
e3b0c442  
df951572  **Resume signal (attention-only optional):** `v8 compaction real-deal green
2a7ce91e  (attention-only): hit_rate=<pct>% ≥ 0.80`
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---

e3b0c442

e3b0c442

a05f99f6  - Memory V3 (rot protection — CRITICAL) → Done

fa7046f4  - Compaction V8 (real-deal session + arch-aware cache hit) → Done
e3b0c442

ecb79751  - V3 (memory) FAIL → CRITICAL — revise description AND consider deferring

e3b0c442  
149d198b  - All 6 protocols are inherently measurement-driven. They cannot run inside the
f3685530    executor wave. The wave-4 plan (04.4-09) lands this runbook + a STATE.md

d6693f93    memory + V2 compaction together (single 1-hour session), V8 compaction last
bb428271    (2-hour standalone, can overlap with daily-driver use).

81e42614  ## V1 — Memory adoption ≥ 60% (Qwen 35B-A3B v3.1)
e3b0c442  
32a4a000  **Fixture:** 20-task batch — pick tasks that present a "would benefit from a note"
9ce48c2c  moment. Examples:
f559a824  - "Find and fix the off-by-one bug in `src/parsers/tokenizer.ts`"
fb642452  - "Add a new endpoint following the existing CRUD convention"
7c6065c6  - "What was that hashline edit pattern again?"
e68975d8  - "We had to use TextEncoder for prefix-hash bytes — explain why"

7db3fbb9  Each task is a fresh session; memory tool enabled (default); project + global scope
8993f73f  clean before task 1, then accumulating.

c4d44de2  1. `pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1` (no `--no-memory`)
1517afd3  2. Run all 20 tasks; record each session's transcript via JSONL telemetry

246002c6     - Did the model `view /memories/...` before the work? (Y/N)
fc4a94af     - Did it write a note via `create` or `str_replace` when a discovery was made? (Y/N — only when applicable)
6e4bedb5  4. Adoption = (sessions with at least one `view` call) / 20
42362f1f  5. **Pass:** adoption ≥ 60%
90b2fbc5  6. **Fail behavior:** revise tool description in plan 04.4-01 per CONTEXT.md
e51a6812     "Calibration protocol: measurement-driven expansion only"; re-run.
e3b0c442  
e68b47eb  **Resume signal:** `v1 memory adoption green: <observed_pct>%` OR
ad0a1a7b  `v1 memory adoption FAIL — observed <pct>%, recommend description revision`

f02f91be  ## V2 (memory) — Memory write discipline ≥ 70% load-bearing

21e67919    jq 'select(.event=="memory.op" and (.["emmy.memory.command"]=="create" or .["emmy.memory.command"]=="str_replace"))' \
b3effd16    > /tmp/v2-write-ops.json
f1b90184  ```

b055479a  - **YES (load-bearing):** API quirk, project convention, hard-won discovery,

ee07f92e  - **NO (narrative):** "then I fixed the bug", already-obvious facts, transient state

a63c5ca4  **Fail behavior:** description didn't sufficiently discourage narrative; expand
410ffaa4  description with sterner phrasing per CONTEXT.md calibration protocol.

43f408ac  **Resume signal:** `v2 memory discipline green: <observed_pct>%` OR
e9349029  `v2 memory discipline FAIL — observed <pct>%, narrative writes: <samples>`
